understanding.goal_understanding: Progress through each KA59 level by moving the selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked '+' is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time; when all goals are covered simultaneously the level reports WIN, and winning the final level completes the run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions shifted the currently selected piece one grid step (3 pixels per cell) in the chosen direction, with collisions depending on object types: walls generally blocked motion, while pushable blocks could be moved if directly adjacent in the movement direction and there was space to push into. Levels could include multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK on an on-screen selectable's exact pixel/grid coordinate switched control to that piece. The movement.*_blocked flags acted only as adjacency hints and did not always perfectly predict whether a move would succeed due to differing boundary/wall behaviors.
